channel.title: CTV News – Queen's Park
channel.description: Queen's Park coverage from CTV News Toronto.

item.title: Toronto's donor-funded consumption sites bracing for fallout of Ontario funding cuts
‘I’m glad you shot the guy:’ Doug Ford praises Vaughan resident who opened fire on intruder
item.description: Premier Doug Ford is congratulating a Vaughan resident who shot an intruder, saying he should have fired more shots at him.

item.title: Ontario premier says electric vehicle mandates putting jobs at risk, urges Quebec, B.C. to scrap policies
item.description: Premier Doug Ford is calling on the premiers of both Quebec and British Columbia to end all electric vehicle mandates, arguing that the policies are “threatening the livelihoods of tens of thousands of Canadian workers.”

item.title: Statues on grounds of Queen’s Park to be wrapped in the wake of being vandalized during protest
item.description: The statues on the ground of Queen’s Park will be covered in temporary wrapping after some of them were vandalized by protesters earlier this month.

item.title: Ford still 'keen' on Niagara amalgamations, plans to review governance in the region
item.description: Ontario Premier Doug Ford said Tuesday he is still keen on amalgamations in the Niagara Region despite not meeting his previously stated threshold for moving forward, though his office later poured some cold water on the idea.

item.title: Ontario may allow more drivers to use HOV lanes
item.description: The Ford government is proposing changes to the Highway Traffic Act that would allow single-occupant vehicles to use High Occupancy Vehicle (HOV) lanes during off-peak hours.

item.title: ‘We got to protect ourselves:’ Doug Ford cites threat from China in defending cabinet secrecy
item.description: Ontario Premier Doug Ford is defending his government’s decision to exclude his office and those of his cabinet ministers from Freedom of Information (FOI) laws, saying the move is necessary to protect the privacy of his constituents and to safeguard information from foreign entities.

item.title: Doug Ford says he wants to livestream bail hearings as he announces new jail
item.description: Ontario Premier Doug Ford said Monday his government is exploring the idea of live-streaming bail hearings to the public.

item.title: Ontario urges federal government to legalize pepper spray for self-defence
item.description: Ontario is calling on the federal government to legally allow people to carry and use pepper spray for self-defence.

item.title: Ford government to end funding for remaining supervised consumption sites: advocates
item.description: Ontario’s remaining supervised consumption sites will be forced to close after Premier Doug Ford’s government informed them Friday that the province is planning to pull their funding, harm reduction advocates say.

item.title: Al-Quds rally in Toronto to proceed as planned despite Ford’s push to stop it, lawyer says
item.description: Premier Doug Ford says he has instructed the province’s attorney general to 'pursue' an injunction this afternoon to stop the planned Al-Quds Day rally in downtown Toronto on Saturday.

item.title: Ontario to give retailers the option of opening on Family Day and Victoria Day
item.description: Stores across Ontario could soon open on Family Day and Victoria Day under a new bill the provincial government plans to introduce.

item.title: Premier and cabinet ministers will be exempt from FOI requests under new legislation
item.description: The Ontario government says it will be introducing legislation to keep secret documents from the premier’s office as well as the offices of his cabinet ministers.

item.title: Make teaching more attractive for tradespeople, teachers' federation tells Ontario
item.description: Ontario must make entering the teaching profession more attractive to skilled tradespeople if it hopes to address a long-standing shortage of technological education teachers and ultimately get more students into the trades, a teachers organization says.

item.title: Simcoe County students express opposition to OSAP cuts by the Ontario government
item.description: High school students in Barrie traded in their books for protest signs on Wednesday to fight the Ontario government’s planned changes to the province’s student financial aid program.
